metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5422
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: MKL1 (gene MRTFA) was detected in synaptic biochemical fractions prepared from rat brain homogenate. Although staining was faint, MKL1 appeared to be enriched in crude synaptic vesicle and PSD fractions as compared to P2 fraction.

Figure 4: Immunostaining of mature cortical neuronal cultures showed endogenous MKL1 (gene MRTFA) was present in both presynapses (co-localization with synaptophysin/SYP) and dendritic spines (co-localization with PSD95/DLG4). There also seems to be ample non-synaptic staining and authors commented on this; "However, not all puncta stained with anti-MKL1 or anti-MKL2 antibodies colocalised with puncta stained with anti-PSD-95 or anti-synaptophysin antibodies.". Experimental description: Results, Figure 3 legend: "Detection of endogenous MKL1 and MKL2 protein in subcellular fractions of rat brains."

antibody:
"Generation of antibodies against MKL1 or MKL2 was as follows. The C-terminal regions of mouse MKL1 (333 amino acid residues) and mouse MKL2 (333 amino acid residues) proteins were bacterially expressed as maltose-binding protein (MBP) fusion proteins and purified using amylose-resin (New England Biolabs, Ipswich, MA, USA). MBP-MKL1 or MBP-MKL2 fusion proteins were conjugated with Freund's adjuvant and subcutaneously injected into guinea pigs five times at 2-week intervals. After sera were incubated with MBP to eliminate immunoglobulins against MBP, specific antibodies were further affinity-purified using MBP-MKL1 or MBP-MKL2 fusion proteins coupled to CNBr-activated Sepharose 4B (GE Healthcare)."

shRNA from ref PMID:20709749 ;
"The myc-tagged mouse MKL2 construct (Myc-MKL2) was generated as
follows. Plasmid mKIAA1243 (carrying the MKL2 gene), provided by the KAZUSA DNA Institute
(Chiba, Japan), was used as a template for site-directed mutagenesis with primer pairs (sense:
5'-GCAGGCTGTCGAATGAGATCTCATCGATAGCTCCAAG-3'; antisense:
5'-CTTGGAGCTATCGATGAGATCTCATTCGACAGCCTGC-3') to create a BglII site upstream of the
initiation codon."

gene nomenclature:
"pLKO.1-MKL1/2 shRNA was a gift from Ron Prywes (Addgene plasmid # 27161)31. pLKO.1-MKL1/2 shRNA shows 100% similarity to rat MKL2 and 95% similarity to rat MKL1."

Nucleotide blast for the Addgene plasmid (CATGGAGCTGGTGGAGAAGAA) confirms the targeted genes are Mrtfa and Mrtfb.
